headings.text: Need a Police Station Rep in Kent?

content: HomePolice Station RatesNew Regulations Effective 22 December 2025Police Station Legal Aid Rates 2025/26The Criminal Legal Aid (General and Remuneration) (Amendment) Regulations 2025 have introduced the most significant overhaul of police station fees in over a decade. Updated: December 7, 2025â€˘ Fact CheckedNew Harmonised FeeÂŁ320.00Universal fixed fee for all 245 schemes (excluding VAT).Previous RatesÂŁ223.52 (National) / ÂŁVaried (London)Escape ThresholdÂŁ650.00New unified threshold. Claims exceeding this amount revert to hourly rates.Effective DateDec 22Applies to all cases with a UFN assigned on or after this date.Understanding the 2025 AmendmentsOn 22 December 2025, the Ministry of Justice implemented the Criminal Legal Aid (General and Remuneration) (Amendment) Regulations 2025 (SI 2025/1251). This legislation fundamentally changes how police station advice and assistance is remunerated in England and Wales.Previously, the legal aid system relied on a complex matrix of 245 separate "schemes", each with its own fixed fee ranging from ÂŁ223.52 (National) to ÂŁ315.86 (Heathrow). This disparity often meant that representatives doing identical work in different counties were paid vastly different amounts.The Key Change: HarmonisationThe 2025 Regulations abolish these regional variations for the fixed fee. There is now a single, harmonised Fixed Fee of ÂŁ320.00 applicable to every police station scheme in England and Wales.Impact Analysis: Winners and Losers The "Winners"Providers in "National" rate areas see the biggest uplift. For schemes like Birmingham, Manchester, Leeds, and most non-London counties, the fee jumps significantly.Old National RateÂŁ223.52New RateÂŁ320.00+ÂŁ96.48 per case (+43%) London & HeathrowFor London schemes, the increase is more modest.Heathrow was previously the highest paid scheme and sees a very slight increase or effective freeze relative to inflation.Heathrow (Old):ÂŁ315.86Brent (Old):ÂŁ241.18New Uniform Rate:ÂŁ320.00The New Escape Fee MechanismThe Escape Fee is a safety valve for complex cases. If the work you do on a caseâ€”calculated at hourly ratesâ€”exceeds a certain threshold, you are paid for the actual time spent rather than the fixed fee.Crucial Update: Alongside the fixed fee harmonisation, the Escape Fee Threshold has also been harmonised to ÂŁ650.00.How to Calculate an Escape Fee (Post-Dec 2025)1. Calculate Your Profit CostsSum up attendance, travel and waiting time using the applicable hourly rates from Schedule 4. Rates vary by category (own solicitor, duty solicitor, unsocial hours, serious offence).Own Solicitor RatesAttendance (London):ÂŁ58.97/hrAttendance (National):ÂŁ54.57/hrTravel/Waiting:ÂŁ30.22/hrDuty Solicitor RatesAttendance (London):ÂŁ58.97/hrAttendance (National):ÂŁ54.57/hrTravel/Wait (London):ÂŁ58.97/hrTravel/Wait (National):ÂŁ54.57/hr2. Compare to Thresholdâś“Total > ÂŁ650.00?You claim the full hourly amount (e.g., ÂŁ750).âś—Total â‰¤ ÂŁ650.00?You claim the Fixed Fee (ÂŁ320.00).Enhanced Rates Also AvailableUnsocial Hours: ÂŁ72.46/hr (attendance + travel/waiting)Serious Offence (London): ÂŁ68.21/hr attendanceSerious Offence (National): ÂŁ62.96/hr attendanceSerious + Unsocial: ÂŁ83.95/hr attendanceUse Our Escape Fee CalculatorKey PointsYou cannot mix the fixed fee and hourly rates. You claim the fixed fee unless your hourly profit costs exceed ÂŁ650.Travel and waiting time are factored into the hourly rate calculation. They are included within the ÂŁ320 fixed fee and are not paid on top unless the case escapes.Mileage (45p per mile for first 10,000 miles, 25p thereafter) is a separate disbursement â€” it is not part of the profit costs calculation.Claiming Multiple FeesA common query is whether multiple fees can be claimed for attending the same client. The rules remain unchanged by the 2025 regulations. The key test is whether the matters are "truly separate". Claim Two Fees If:Different Occasions: Offences committed on clearly different dates.Unconnected Matters: Arrested for Assault today, but also questioned about a historic Burglary from 3 months ago.Separate Investigations: Matters that would likely not be joined on the same indictment. Single Fee Only If:Same Incident: Multiple charges arising from one event (e.g., Drink Drive + Assault PC).Linked Series: 5 shopliftings from the same Tesco over a week.Multiple Interviews: Interviewed at 10am and again at 4pm for the same matter (claims as one attendance).Transitional Arrangements (UFN Dates)The rate you can claim is strictly determined by the Unique File Number (UFN) date assigned to the case.Before 22 Dec 2025Must claim Old Rates (e.g., ÂŁ223.52 National).On/After 22 Dec 2025Claims New Rate (ÂŁ320.00).Training Guides & ResourcesTraining guides, Rep Wiki, and professional resources â€” all freeBrowse All ResourcesWhat's IncludedComplete "How to Become a Rep" guideComplete "Get Work as a Rep" guideFull Rep Wiki knowledge baseAll resources & templatesWhy It's FreeNo payment requiredFull access for all usersCommunity-driven contentRegularly updated guidesBrowse All ResourcesQuick Rate LookupSearch 245 schemes to confirm they fall under the new unified
